summary: Top 6 +EV picks · NBA Props (HOU @ LAL) + NHL Props (Utah @ VGK)

picks.reasoning: Model projects Thompson at 5.8 assists — a clean 0.3 above the 5.5 line. The sharp market (Pinnacle) has already priced the Over at -138, reflecting 55%+ true probability, but Fanduel is sitting soft at +104 — creating nearly 10% edge on the correct side. Thompson has been operating as Houston's primary distributor, and against a Lakers defense that concedes clean kickouts from the elbow, his assist rate climbs in games where Sengun is the hub.

Model median of 3.0 shots sits a full half-shot below the 3.5 line, with the distribution centered squarely at 3. Model win probability for Under 3.5 comes in at ~63%, making RI's -160 a +2.7% EV play. Utah's defensive structure is disciplined in limiting center-ice access, which tends to keep Eichel from generating extra looks — he's a creator first, not a high-volume shooter.

Thompson's model median sits at 18.2 — a narrow miss below the 18.5 line. RI's -104 juice is about as thin as it gets for a player prop this size, letting the small edge through. In a game where Thompson's primary value is playmaking (see pick #1), his scoring role is slightly secondary, keeping him right at or below the 18.5 ceiling.

Model puts Reaves at 4.0 assists — a half-assist under the 4.5 market line. Reaves is a secondary distributor on a team where LeBron and Davis handle primary playmaking. At RI -134, the juice is acceptable given the model's 56% win probability.

Sengun's model median of 22.6 barely clears the 22.5 line — a coin-flip with the model just tipping to Over. He faces Deandre Ayton at center, a soft post defender Sengun can exploit in isolation and off the pick-and-roll. At RI -110 this is a standard-juice, model-supported spot.

LeBron's model median of 7.3 falls below the 7.5 line. The sharp market (Pinnacle) prices the Under at -141, confirming the directional lean — RI at -122 offers meaningfully better juice on the same side. With Sengun anchoring Houston's interior, LeBron faces competition for defensive boards and may settle into his playmaking role over rebounding.
